title: Abnormal Chest X-ray & Advanced Imaging Interpretation Quiz

questions.text: Which of the following chest X-ray findings is most characteristic of advanced COPD?

answers.text: Hyperlucent lung fields with flattened diaphragms
Kerley B lines at the lung bases
Air bronchogram within a consolidated lobe

questions.text: The radiographic term 'air bronchogram' describes:

answers.text: Collapsed bronchi displaced toward the mediastinum
Air-filled bronchi visible as dark branching lines against opaque consolidated lung
Dilated, thick-walled bronchi that fail to taper
Fluid-filled bronchi seen on a lateral decubitus view

questions.text: A key radiographic sign that helps differentiate atelectasis from consolidation is:

answers.text: Displacement of fissures toward the collapsed lung

questions.text: Blunting of the costophrenic angle on an upright chest X-ray is most suggestive of:
Which of the following radiographic findings is pathognomonic for a pneumothorax?

answers.text: A visible visceral pleural line with absent peripheral vascular markings
Wedge-shaped opacity with the base against the pleura

questions.text: In a tension pneumothorax, tracheal deviation is expected to be:

answers.text: There is no tracheal deviation in tension pneumothorax

questions.text: Kerley B lines on a chest X-ray are most associated with:
The 'batwing' or 'butterfly' distribution of infiltrates on chest X-ray is classic for:
Which of the following best describes the radiographic appearance of ARDS?

answers.text: Bilateral diffuse ground glass and fluffy opacities
Visceral pleural line with absent vascular markings

questions.text: A wedge-shaped pleural-based opacity on chest X-ray (Hampton hump) is most suggestive of:
The 'thumb sign' on a lateral neck radiograph is characteristic of:
The 'steeple sign' on a lateral neck radiograph is characteristic of:
Which patient population is at highest risk for foreign body aspiration?
Paradoxical chest wall movement (inward during inspiration) is the hallmark clinical finding of:
Which advanced imaging modality is considered the gold standard for diagnosing pulmonary embolism?
A V/Q scan reveals normal ventilation but absent perfusion in a segmental distribution. This mismatched pattern most likely indicates:
Which of the following is a key safety consideration for mechanically ventilated patients undergoing an MRI?

answers.text: MRI-safe intravenous contrast must be used
A fluidic (gas-powered) ventilator should be used instead of an electronic one
The patient must be paralyzed for the duration of the scan
All metal equipment is safe as long as it is titanium

questions.text: Which gas cylinders should be used inside an MRI suite?
The 'tram track' or 'signet ring' sign on CT imaging is diagnostic of:
A patient with sudden-onset dyspnea, tachycardia, and hemoptysis has a normal chest X-ray. What is the most appropriate next imaging step?

answers.text: CT pulmonary angiography or V/Q scan
